---
title: "Sport Popularity Over Time"
output: html_document
---
```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(readxl)

# Load and clean data (same as your main file)
df <- read_xlsx("Summer_Olympic_medallists_1896-2008.xlsx")

df.summer <- df %>%
  filter(!Sport %in% c("Ice Hockey", "Skating")) %>%
  mutate(NOC = recode(NOC, "URS" = "RUS"))

df.medals <- df.summer %>%
  distinct(Edition, Event, NOC, Medal, Gender, Sport, Discipline, Event_gender)

# Build and render the plot
p <- df.medals %>%
  group_by(Edition, Sport) %>%
  summarise(Total = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Edition, y = Total, color = Sport)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Sport Popularity Over Time",
       x = "Edition",
       y = "Total Medals")

ggplotly(p)
```
